subject: Project Management
title: The Agile Manifesto
description: A fast breakdown of The Agile Manifesto for builders.

hook.text: In February 2001, 17 developers met at a ski lodge in Utah. They wrote a manifesto.

buildup.text: They were frustrated with bloated plans, rigid processes, and projects that delivered wrong.

discovery.text: Four values emerged: people over process, working software over docs, collaboration over contracts.

twist.text: The manifesto was intentionally vague — no method, no certification, just principles.

climax.text: Within a decade, Agile spread from software to marketing, HR, and even the military.

punchline.text: Seventeen rebels at a ski lodge rewrote the rules.

quiz.question: Where was the Agile Manifesto written?
options: At a ski lodge in Utah
At a tech conference in San Francisco
